project: Model Atlas — Phase 1 Turbopack Tracing Warning Cleanup
description: Eliminate or narrowly document the Next.js/Turbopack NFT whole-project filesystem tracing warning caused by runtime content loaders (notably glossary-pages.ts) during next build, by scoping filesystem access to src/content, decoupling fs scanners from the server-component import graph, and adding a build regression gate—without changing Phase 1 routes, search, or content scope.

context.customerAsk: Phase 1 repair: address the current Next/Turbopack production-build warning caused by broad filesystem tracing from content loaders, currently surfacing through src/lib/content/glossary-pages.ts during next build. Narrow filesystem access so tracing stays scoped to the content tree, or add the smallest acceptable documented containment if the warning cannot be eliminated cleanly in Phase 1. Do not broaden site scope or add Phase 2 content; the goal is a cleaner and more deterministic Phase 1 build.
context.problem: Runtime content loaders under src/lib/content use node:fs and process.cwd() to discover MDX pages, registry JSON, and locale messages. When imported into App Router pages or MDX server components, Turbopack NFT may trace the whole project filesystem and emit a maintainer-visible warning during next build even though the build succeeds.
context.solution: Add a build regression test for the Turbopack NFT tracing warning, centralize content filesystem roots under src/content, keep filesystem scanners behind async or dynamic server-only boundaries, resolve or consolidate glossary-pages.ts out of the production import graph, and apply minimal next.config tracing containment plus progress.txt documentation only if the warning cannot be eliminated cleanly in Phase 1.

acceptanceCriteria: bun run build exits 0 without printing the Turbopack NFT unintentional whole-project filesystem tracing warning from content loader import chains.

If the warning persists after scoping, progress.txt documents the residual warning with import trace, root cause, attempted fix, and follow-up recommendation, plus minimal Turbopack containment limited to src/content when applicable.
Runtime content loaders resolve paths through a shared content-root helper targeting src/content, not scattered process.cwd() joins in the production graph.
App Router pages and MDX server components do not statically import modules that invoke filesystem APIs at module evaluation time.
glossary-pages.ts is consolidated with existing glossary loading or removed from the production server import graph.
Phase 1 route smoke tests, glossary/tag loader tests, and static route verification pass without URL or content changes.
Typecheck, lint, and tests pass via make ci.

userStories.title: Add build regression gate for Turbopack tracing warning
userStories.description: As a CI maintainer, I want an automated test that fails when next build reintroduces the Turbopack NFT whole-project tracing warning so regressions are caught before merge.
acceptanceCriteria: A Bun test runs bun run build from the repository root with .next removed beforehand and expects exit code 0.
The test fails if combined build stdout/stderr matches the known Turbopack NFT whole-project filesystem tracing warning pattern associated with content loader import chains.
The test comment documents the warning substring(s) it guards against for future Next.js message updates.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Centralize content filesystem roots under src/content
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want all runtime content loaders to resolve filesystem paths through one shared content-root helper so Turbopack tracing targets the content tree instead of the whole repository.
acceptanceCriteria: A shared content-paths module exports stable roots for src/content, docs, glossary, modules, registry, and messages derived from the project directory.
glossary-pages.ts, pages.ts, registry.ts, ui-messages.ts, glossary-page.ts, module-page.ts, and related loaders consume the shared roots; tests may still override via options or fixtures.
Existing glossary, pages, registry, and UI message loader tests pass with unchanged behavioral expectations.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Keep filesystem scanners out of the static server-component import graph
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want App Router pages and MDX server components to import only pure helpers or thin async facades so Turbopack does not trace broad filesystem access through the React server graph.
acceptanceCriteria: Route modules under src/app and server MDX components such as DerivedRelatedDocs do not have static import chains that evaluate node:fs APIs at module load time.
Filesystem scanning for glossary, tag, architecture, and per-page loaders occurs inside async functions or dynamically imported server-only modules.
Pure modules content-hrefs.ts, related-docs.ts, and registry-runtime.ts remain free of filesystem imports.
/docs/glossary, /docs/glossary/token, /docs/modules/grouped-query-attention, /tags, and /tags/attention render the same published Phase 1 content as before.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Resolve glossary-pages.ts tracing surface
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want the loader called out in the Turbopack warning removed from the production server graph or consolidated with the existing glossary index path.
acceptanceCriteria: Production App Router and MDX import graphs no longer statically depend on glossary-pages.ts, or listPublishedGlossaryPages is consolidated with loadPublishedGlossaryEntries so only one glossary scanner exists in the server graph.
Glossary index behavior is unchanged: published token entry appears with title, summary, and /docs/glossary/token URL; draft entries remain excluded.
glossary-pages.test.ts continues to pass after any consolidation.
The build regression test no longer detects a tracing warning whose import trace includes glossary-pages.ts.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Apply Turbopack containment fallback or document residual warning
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want either a warning-free build or the smallest documented containment when scoping alone cannot eliminate the Turbopack NFT warning in Phase 1.
acceptanceCriteria: If stories 002–004 eliminate the warning, no additional Turbopack tracing config beyond existing turbopack.root is required and progress.txt needs no residual-warning entry.

If the warning persists after scoping, add the smallest acceptable tracing containment in next.config.ts limited to src/content and record the import trace, root cause, attempted fixes, and follow-up in progress.txt.
bun run build exits 0 in either outcome.
The build regression test passes, or—only when documentation is the chosen outcome—the test asserts the documented accepted residual is absent from stdout while containment config is present.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Verify Phase 1 CI and discovery surface unchanged
userStories.description: As a Phase 1 maintainer, I want full quality gates green and discovery routes unchanged after tracing cleanup.
acceptanceCriteria: make ci exits 0 running lint, typecheck, test, build with Phase 1 static route verification, and validate-data.
scripts/verify-phase-1-static-routes.ts passes against the production build manifest.
No new Phase 2 routes, registry records, or content pages are added.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
